title: After A Tempestuous Voyage. (At Tynemouth Priory)
content:
As slow I climb the cliff's ascending side,
    Much musing on the track of terror past,
    When o'er the dark wave rode the howling blast,
    Pleased I look back, and view the tranquil tide
    That laves the pebbled shore: and now the beam
    Of evening smiles on the gray battlement,
    And yon forsaken tower that time has rent:
    The lifted oar far off with transient gleam
    Is touched, and hushed is all the billowy deep!
    Soothed by the scene, thus on tired Nature's breast
    A stillness slowly steals, and kindred rest;
    While sea-sounds lull her, as she sinks to sleep,
    Like melodies that mourn upon the lyre,
    Waked by the breeze, and, as they mourn, expire!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Lisle Bowles represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of William Lisle Bowles's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
